response.task: Define project scope from stakeholder requirements, estimate effort using historical data and complexity analysis, then create a realistic schedule with risk buffers and dependency chains.

solution_steps.title: Gather & Prioritize Requirements
solution_steps.description: Collect all stakeholder inputs, classify them by business value and urgency, and produce a ranked requirement list.

solution_steps.title: Set Scope Boundaries
solution_steps.description: Draft an explicit scope statement that lists in‑scope deliverables and out‑of‑scope items to prevent creep.

solution_steps.title: Create Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
solution_steps.description: Break the scope into hierarchical tasks linked to requirements, forming the basis for estimation.

solution_steps.title: Estimate Effort with Historical Data
solution_steps.description: Apply complexity‑weighted analogies from past projects to assign person‑days to each WBS element.

solution_steps.title: Map Dependencies & Identify Critical Path
solution_steps.description: Build a dependency graph, determine sequencing, and highlight activities that drive overall duration.

solution_steps.title: Build Schedule with Risk Buffers
solution_steps.description: Generate a Gantt‑style timeline, adding contingency time on critical path tasks and aligning resources accordingly.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'project_management', 'task': 'Define project scope from stakeholder requirements, estimate effort using historical data and complexity analysis, then create a realistic schedule with risk buffers and dependency chains.', 'execution_trace': {'steps': [{'action': 'gather and categorize stakeholder requirements by priority', 'result': 'success'}, {'action': 'define scope boundaries with explicit in-scope and out-of-scope items', 'result': 'success'}, {'action': 'decompose scope into work breakdown structure tasks', 'result': 'success'}, {'action': 'estimate effort using complexity-weighted historical comparisons', 'result': 'success'}, {'action': 'identify task dependencies and critical path', 'result': 'success'}, {'action': 'build schedule with risk buffers on critical path items', 'result': 'success'}], 'agents': ['requirements_gatherer', 'scope_definer', 'wbs_builder', 'effort_estimator', 'dependency_mapper', 'scheduler'], 'strategy': 'sequential'}, 'outcome': {'success': True, 'quality': 0.87}}

SOLUTION:
The project scope and schedule are defined through a sequential planning process:
1. **Gather & Prioritize Requirements** – Collect stakeholder inputs, classify them by business value and urgency, producing a ranked requirement list.
2. **Set Scope Boundaries** – Draft an explicit scope statement that lists in‑scope deliverables and out‑of‑scope items to prevent creep.
3. **Create Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)** – Break the scope into hierarchical tasks linked to requirements, forming the basis for estimation.
4. **Estimate Effort with Historical Data** – Apply complexity‑weighted analogies from past projects to assign person‑days to each WBS element.
5. **Map Dependencies & Identify Critical Path** – Build a dependency graph, determine sequencing, and highlight activities that drive overall duration.
6. **Build Schedule with Risk Buffers** – Generate a Gantt‑style timeline, adding contingency time on critical path tasks and aligning resources accordingly.

This approach follows the PMBOK/PRINCE2 methodology and aligns with the successful execution trace provided.
